title: Resolve linked finding on done
goal: Add a resolveFinding(cwd, id) mutation to the findings store and have mini done --apply flip a completed phase's linked finding (Phase.fromFinding) to resolved, tolerating an already-resolved or missing finding; mini undo reopens it to stay consistent. A manual resolve command and re-review semantics are out of scope (settled in discuss).

steps.title: resolveFinding/reopenFinding in findings store
steps.detail: src/state/findingsStore.ts: private setFindingStatus(cwd,id,status) derives origin phase from id, reads only that file, flips one entry status, re-serializes; exported resolveFinding/reopenFinding return whether changed. Unit tests for unhappy paths (malformed id, missing file, unknown id, already-target status -> silent no-op, no throw) and happy (open<->resolved keeps reviewedAt/body).

steps.title: done resolves linked finding before commit
steps.detail: src/commands/done.ts: in shared finalizePhaseSideEffects call resolveFinding(cwd, phase.fromFinding) before commitPhaseWork, only when fromFinding set, so rewritten findings file lands in the phase commit. Test: phase with fromFinding flips finding to resolved; phase without is unchanged; missing finding does not break done.

steps.title: undo reopens linked finding
steps.detail: src/commands/undo.ts: after restorePrev, for phases that are done in current and exist in prev as non-done and have fromFinding, call reopenFinding. Skip phases absent in prev. Test: undo over done->planned reopens; undo over an added phase leaves finding untouched; reopen of already-open is a no-op.

steps.title: Build and full test suite green
steps.detail: npm run build and npm test pass; verifies integration of steps 1-3 and no regression.

autoCommit.subject: Phase 159: Resolve linked finding on done
